title: Routing priority of non-cellular over cellular
abstract: Various aspects of the present disclosure generally relate to wireless communication. In some aspects, a user equipment (UE) may receive policy information that specifies radio access technology (RAT) priorities for route selection, including a RAT priority of non-cellular over cellular. The UE may establish access for an application over a route that is selected based at least in part on the RAT priority. Numerous other aspects are described.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An apparatus for wireless communication at a user equipment (UE), comprising: a memory; and one or more processors operatively coupled to the memory, the memory and the one or more processors configured to: receive policy information that specifies radio access technology (RAT) priorities for route selection, including a RAT priority of non-cellular over cellular; and establish access for an application over a route that is selected based at least in part on the RAT priority. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the RAT priority of non-cellular over cellular is available if the UE is to establish access from a visited network. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the selected route is a non-cellular over cellular route, and wherein the one or more processors are configured to establish access by setting up a protocol data unit (PDU) session and setting up an internet protocol security protocol (IPSec) tunnel over the PDU session. 4. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the PDU session is set up with a visited network subscriber identity module (SIM) or credential of the UE, and the IPSec tunnel is set up with a home network SIM or credential of the UE. 5. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the PDU session is set up by accessing internet protocol connectivity of a visited network. 6. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the IPSec tunnel is set up to an evolved packet data gateway (ePDG). 7. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the IPSec tunnel is setup to a non-Third Generation Partnership Project (non-3GPP) interworking function (N3IWF). 8. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to select the route according to a RAT priority order of non-cellular, non-cellular over cellular, and cellular. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the application is one of a voice application, a video application, an instant messaging application, or a virtual private networking application. 10. An apparatus for wireless communication at a network node, comprising: a memory; and one or more processors operatively coupled to the memory, the memory and the one or more processors configured to: generate policy information that specifies radio access technology (RAT) priorities for user equipment (UE) route selection, including a RAT priority of non- cellular over cellular; and transmit the policy information to the UE. 11. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the network node is a policy server or a UE route selection policy device. 12. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the network node is an access network discovery and selection function device. 13. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the policy information specifies a RAT priority order of non-cellular, non-cellular over cellular, and cellular. 14. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the policy information specifies that the RAT priority of non-cellular over cellular is available if the UE is to establish access from a visited network. 15. A method of wireless communication performed by a user equipment (UE), comprising: receiving policy information that specifies radio access technology (RAT) priorities for route selection, including a RAT priority of non-cellular over cellular; and establishing access for an application over a route that is selected based at least in part on the RAT priority. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the RAT priority of non-cellular over cellular is available if the UE is to establish access from a visited network. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein the selected route is a non-cellular over cellular route, and wherein establishing access includes setting up a protocol data unit (PDU) session and setting up an internet protocol security protocol (IPSec) tunnel over the PDU session. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the PDU session is set up with a visited network subscriber identity module (SIM) or credential of the UE, and the IPSec tunnel is set up with a home network SIM or credential of the UE. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the PDU session is set up by accessing internet protocol connectivity of a visited network. 20. The method of claim 17, wherein the IPSec tunnel is set up to an evolved packet data gateway (ePDG). 21. The method of claim 17, wherein the IPSec tunnel is setup to a non-Third Generation Partnership Project (non-3GPP) interworking function (N3IWF) 22. The method of claim 15, further comprising selecting the route according to a RAT priority order of non-cellular, non-cellular over cellular, and cellular. 23. The method of claim 15, wherein the application is one of a voice application, a video application, an instant messaging application, or a virtual private networking application. 24. A method of wireless communication performed by a network node, comprising: generating policy information that specifies radio access technology (RAT) priorities for user equipment (UE) route selection, including a RAT priority of non-cellular over cellular; and transmitting the policy information to the UE. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the network node is a policy server or a UE route selection policy device. 26. The method of claim 24, wherein the network node is an access network discovery and selection function device. 27. The method of claim 24, wherein the policy information specifies a RAT priority order of non-cellular, non-cellular over cellular, and cellular. 28. The method of claim 24, wherein the policy information specifies that the RAT priority of non-cellular over cellular is available if the UE is to establish access from a visited network.
